context: When you meet someone in Spain - a host, a guide, a shopkeeper - a few words about yourself break the ice. You don't need to hold a conversation; you just need to say who you are and set the expectation that your Spanish is basic.
explanation: Start with the three you'll lead with: "me llamo" (my name is) plus your name, "mucho gusto" (nice to meet you), and "soy de" (I am from) plus your country. Together they introduce you in one breath.

examples.en: My name is {name}. Nice to meet you.

phrases.literal: How many years do you have?
